context: Vengo de la tienda con las bolsas.
english: of / from / I'm coming from the store with the bags.

context: La mesa está en el centro de la sala.
english: the (feminine) / The table is in the center of the room.

context: Creo que mañana va a llover.
english: that / which / I think that it's going to rain tomorrow.

context: El perro duerme en el sofá.
english: the (masculine) / The dog sleeps on the sofa.

context: Vivo en un apartamento cerca del trabajo.
english: in / at / I live in an apartment near work.

context: Compré pan y leche en el supermercado.
english: and / I bought bread and milk at the supermarket.

context: Voy a la casa de mi madre esta tarde.
english: to / at / I'm going to my mother's house this afternoon.

context: Los niños juegan en el parque todos los días.
english: the (masc. plural) / The children play in the park every day.

context: Ella se levanta temprano todos los días.
english: reflexive pronoun / herself, himself, themselves / She gets up early every day.

context: La llave del coche está sobre la mesa.
english: of the / from the / The car key is on the table.

context: Las llaves están encima de la silla.
english: the (fem. plural) / The keys are on top of the chair.

context: Tengo un gato y dos perros en casa.
english: a / an (masculine) / I have a cat and two dogs at home.

context: Gracias por todo lo que has hecho.
english: for / because of / by / Thank you for everything you've done.

context: ¿Quieres café con leche o solo?
english: with / Do you want coffee with milk or black?

context: No tengo hambre, comí hace poco.
english: no / not / I'm not hungry, I ate a little while ago.

context: Necesito una chaqueta para el frío.
english: a / an (feminine) / I need a jacket for the cold.

context: Dejó su teléfono en la oficina otra vez.
english: his / her / your (formal) / their / He left his phone at the office again.

context: Este regalo es para ti, con mucho cariño.
english: for / in order to / This gift is for you, with much love.

context: Mi madre es la persona más importante en mi vida.
english: is / My mother is the most important person in my life.

context: Llegamos al aeropuerto justo a tiempo.
english: to the / at the / We arrived at the airport just in time.

context: No lo sabía, me enteré hoy.
english: it / him / the (neuter) / I didn't know it, I found out today.

context: Como siempre, llegó tarde a la reunión.
english: like / as / how / As always, he arrived late to the meeting.

context: Necesito dormir más, estoy muy cansado.
english: more / most / I need to sleep more, I'm very tired.

context: ¿Prefieres té o café por la mañana?
english: or / Do you prefer tea or coffee in the morning?

context: Me gusta el plan, pero necesito pensarlo.
english: but / I like the plan, but I need to think about it.

context: Recogió sus cosas y se fue sin decir nada.
english: his / her / their (plural) / He picked up his things and left without saying anything.

context: Le dije la verdad porque confío en él.
english: him / her / you (indirect object) / I told him the truth because I trust him.

context: Mi hermana ha vivido en París tres años.
english: has (auxiliary) / My sister has lived in Paris for three years.

context: Me duele la cabeza desde esta mañana.
english: me / myself (reflexive) / My head has been hurting since this morning.

context: Si tienes hambre, hay fruta en la cocina.
english: if / whether / If you're hungry, there's fruit in the kitchen.
